title: Windows: selecting a clip copies but never pastes — foreground window must be re-activated
description: On Windows, hiding the focused always-on-top popup does not reliably return focus to the previous window (unlike KWin), and the one-shot powershell SendKeys has 1-3s cold-start latency and types into whatever happens to be foreground — often nothing. Fix like Ditto: capture GetForegroundWindow() before showing the popup, and on paste SetForegroundWindow(target) + keybd_event Ctrl+V, via a persistent PowerShell helper process (P/Invoke, no native modules, no cold start). Alt-tap fallback for the SetForegroundWindow foreground-lock.

title: naiveStripHtml drops line breaks — pre-migration html clips paste as one line
description:
The tag-strip fallback for html clips without stored content_text removes block tags (
,
  • ,
    ,

    ) without emitting newlines, so multi-line Discord copies paste as a single line. Make it block-aware and unit-test it.
close_reason: Verified live on host: copying A, B, A-again leaves a single A row at the top of history. htmlToText covered by unit tests (92 passing) including multi-line digest case.

title: HTML clips paste raw markup into plain-text apps — write both clipboard flavors
description: copyClipToSystem writes {html: content, text: content} — the text flavor is the raw markup, so apps that take text/plain (Discord, terminals, editors) paste HTML soup. Fix: capture the plain-text flavor alongside HTML (new content_text column, wl-paste --type text), and on copy write {html, text: contentText || naiveStrip(html)} so behavior matches a native copy. The existing Save HTML settings toggle remains the feature switch to disable HTML capture entirely.
close_reason: Dual-flavor capture and paste verified on host: content_text stored for dual-flavor sources, NULL + naive-strip fallback for html-only sources, clipboard.write presents both flavors. Save HTML toggle remains the kill-switch.

title: Popup position modes have no visible effect — popup always lands mid-secondary-monitor
description: All four position modes land in the same place. Suspects: (1) KWin placement policy overriding setPosition at map time (fix: re-assert position after show), (2) stale X11 pointer under XWayland for mouse mode, (3) popupPosition not persisting. Diagnose empirically per mode via xwininfo.
close_reason: KWin helper script: focus-restore before paste injection (works for Wayland-native targets like VSCode), true-cursor popup placement, center-on-cursor-screen. Verified on host: popup maps at true cursor position while X11 pointer is stale elsewhere.

title: Paste lands in wrong window — must re-activate the pre-popup window before injecting
description: After popup hides, KWin's focus restore is unpredictable (pasted into 'Messages' instead of focused VSCode). Capture the active window id (xdotool getactivewindow) before showing the popup and explicitly re-activate it (--sync) before injecting Ctrl+V.
close_reason: KWin helper script: focus-restore before paste injection (works for Wayland-native targets like VSCode), true-cursor popup placement, center-on-cursor-screen. Verified on host: popup maps at true cursor position while X11 pointer is stale elsewhere.

title: Popup shows stale clip list — never reloads after startup; image previews broken
description: popup.ts init() loads clips once at window creation; the window is hidden/shown afterwards so users never see new captures. Fix: reload on visibilitychange→visible, reset search/selection, refocus. Also image clips render data:image/png;base64,<content> where content is a file PATH — attach real base64 preview in clips:get-recent.
close_reason: Popup refresh verified live on the host: clip count updated from stale 19 to 30 on show, marker string captured post-startup appears in DB and list. Clear History wired via clips:clear IPC + native dialog; clearAllClips covered by unit tests (84 passing).

title: GPU process crashes (exit 139) under forced X11 backend — app falls back to software rendering
description: With ozone-platform=x11 on the Bazzite/KDE Wayland host, Chromium's GPU process segfaults repeatedly at startup and rendering falls back to software. Cosmetic/log noise for now. Consider app.disableHardwareAcceleration() on Linux or investigating driver combo.
notes: Escalated: not just log noise — no window (popup/settings/about) renders at all under forced x11 backend on this host. Taskbar entry appears but surface never paints.
close_reason: Root cause found: Electron 42 regression on KDE Wayland (Bazzite) — no window is ever composited under the native Wayland backend, and under forced x11 the GPU process crash-loops and no X11 windows are created. Verified with a minimal red-window repro script: invisible on 42, visible on 33. Fixed by pinning Electron back to ^33 (runs via XWayland; windows, positioning, and cursor queries all work). Verified popup visibly on screen via screenshot.

title: Wayland: clipboard capture broken — force XWayland (ozone x11) backend
description: Electron 42 defaults to native Wayland, where clipboard reads require window focus, so background polling never captures. Force --ozone-platform=x11 on Linux; KWin/Mutter sync the Wayland clipboard to X11, so polling works again. Also restores window positioning for the popup.
close_reason: Fixed and verified end-to-end on Bazzite/KDE Wayland: wl-paste watcher captures live copies into the DB; KDE shortcut registered and activated via kglobalaccel D-Bus without re-login.

title: Wayland: globalShortcut false-positives, KDE/GNOME fallback never engages
description: globalShortcut.register() returns true on Wayland sessions but never fires (compositor owns shortcuts). Detect a Wayland session and report registration failure so the DE-level shortcut fallback in desktop-shortcut.ts actually runs. Also add dbus-send/systemctl fallbacks to reload kglobalaccel (host lacks qdbus).
close_reason: Fixed and verified end-to-end on Bazzite/KDE Wayland: wl-paste watcher captures live copies into the DB; KDE shortcut registered and activated via kglobalaccel D-Bus without re-login.

title: Core: Settings window
description:
Build a settings window for configuring the application.

Settings:
- Hotkey binding (default: Ctrl+Backquote)
- History depth (default: 100)
- Retention period in days (default: 30)
- Auto-start on login toggle
- Save images toggle
- Save HTML toggle
- Save binary files toggle

All settings are persisted to ~/.CtrlC/config.toml and re-read on app start.

Acceptance: All settings save correctly, persist across restarts, and take effect immediately where applicable.
close_reason: Closed

title: Core: Popup window with search and keyboard shortcuts
description:
Build the popup window that displays recent clipboard items.

Features:
- Search field to filter clips by content
- List of recent clips with preview (text snippet or image thumbnail)
- Type badge (TEXT, HTML, IMAGE, BINARY, FILE)
- Number key 1-5 to quickly copy first 5 clips
- Up/Down arrow keys to navigate list
- Enter to copy selected clip
- Escape to close popup without pasting
- Ctrl+V to copy as-is (preserves HTML formatting)
- Ctrl+Shift+V to paste as plain text (strips HTML)
- Click on any clip to copy it

Dark matrix-green theme matching Serious Geese aesthetic.

Acceptance: Popup opens on hotkey, search filters results, keyboard shortcuts work, clicking a clip copies it to clipboard.
close_reason: Closed

title: Core: Global hotkey to open popup
description:
Register a global hotkey (default: Ctrl+`) that opens the clipboard history popup at the current mouse position.

Requirements:
- Hotkey is configurable via settings
- Popup opens at mouse cursor position
- Pressing hotkey again when popup is open should refresh the list
- Works across all windows/applications

Acceptance: Pressing the hotkey from any application opens the popup centered on the screen (on Wayland) or at cursor position (on Windows/macOS).
close_reason: Closed

title: UX issues: settings close button, hotkey display, menu bar, ESC dismiss, window icons
description:
Several UX issues found during testing:

**Settings Window:**
- "Close" button overlays content and looks bad - should be in header or removed
- "Toggle popup" hotkey text is truncated ("CommandOrControl+Backqu") and too long
- "Appearance" section title doesn't match its contents (should be "Retention" or "General")
- Hotkey recording has no cancel mechanism - Esc and X button needed
- Window icon shows "W" instead of app icon

**Popup:**
- "Esc to dismiss" shown but Esc doesn't actually dismiss the popup

**Menu Bar:**
- Edit, View, Window menu items present but unused - should be removed

**Platform Display:**
- Hotkey display should show "Ctrl" for Linux/Windows, "Cmd" for macOS instead of "CommandOrControl"
close_reason: Closed

title: Core: Implement clipboard capture with auto-save
description:
Build the background clipboard listener that auto-captures every clipboard change. Supports text, HTML (rich text), images, and binary content.

Requirements:
- Listen for clipboard changes across all apps
- Auto-save to SQLite database on change
- Deduplicate: same content hash only stored once
- Store content in ~/.CtrlC/Clips/<hash>.<ext>
- Track content type (text/html/image/binary/file)
- Track source app name if available

Storage layout:
- ~/.CtrlC/.config/cutc.db — SQLite (clip metadata + content hash)
- ~/.CtrlC/Clips/ — raw content files (images, binaries, HTML)
- ~/.CtrlC/config.toml — user configuration

Acceptance: Every clipboard change is captured, deduplicated, and stored. The database query returns clips ordered by recency.
close_reason: Closed

title: PoC: Verify Wayland clipboard access is feasible
description: BELOW ARE THE PO FINDINGS **Result: PARTIALLY VIABLE — significant Wayland limitations discovered.** **What works on Wayland (confirmed via wl-clipboard test):** - Bold reads text and HTML clipboard content - Bold reads HTML MIME type - writes HTML content - text/html text/plain text/plain;charset=utf-8 TEXT STRING UTF8_STRING shows available clipboard formats - watch mode works for listening to clipboard changes **Electron Wayland findings (from Electron blog March 2026):** - Electron 33.x (our current version) does NOT have native Wayland support — runs through XWayland - Electron 38.2+ has native Wayland support - Electron 41+ has full Wayland CSD support **Critical Wayland restrictions for our app:** 1. is NOT supported — we cannot get mouse coordinates 2. is NOT supported — we cannot position popup at cursor 3. is more restricted but still available 4. Window activation behavior differs per compositor (GNOME shows notification, KDE flashes icon) **Recommendation: Upgrade to Electron 42 (latest stable)** The upgrade is needed regardless because: - Electron 33 is outdated and lacks security fixes - Electron 38+ has native Wayland support - Electron 42 is the current stable version **Workaround for popup positioning on Wayland:** - Center popup on screen (we already have this as fallback) - Use portal-based approach for cursor position (complex, per-compositor) - Accept that popup will not follow cursor on Wayland — this is a known limitation **Workaround for clipboard on Wayland:** - Electron's clipboard API works in native Wayland mode (no workaround needed) - Electron 33 via XWayland also works but with potential rendering quirks - wl-clipboard confirms clipboard data IS accessible on Wayland **PoC Verdict: Proceed, but upgrade Electron version. Popup positioning on Wayland will be screen-centered, not cursor-positioned. This is an acceptable trade-off.**
close_reason: Closed

title: macOS: paste beeps instead of pasting; focus never returns to target app
description: On macOS, showing the popup activates the CtrlC app, stealing focus from the app the user was in (e.g. TextEdit). captureForegroundWindow (win-paste helper) and restorePreviousFocus (KWin helper) are both no-ops on darwin, so focus is never handed back. When synthesizePaste fires Cmd+V, CtrlC is still frontmost and has no text field, so macOS beeps and nothing pastes; the clip only lands on the clipboard. Fix: call app.hide() before the Cmd+V keystroke so macOS returns focus to the previously-frontmost app (standard clipboard-manager behavior).

title: Crash: 'write EIO' uncaught exception when launching terminal's pty dies — console.* writes to dead stdout
description: App launched from a terminal that later closes leaves process.stdout a dead socket. Every console.log/warn/error then emits EIO with no 'error' listener, becoming an uncaught main-process exception (error dialog on second-instance Ctrl+` handler at main.ts:64). Worse, clipboard.ts wl-paste exit handler logs BEFORE startPolling(), so the EIO throw kills the polling fallback and capture stops silently. Fix: attach no-op 'error' handlers to process.stdout/stderr at startup; reorder exit handler to start polling before logging.
close_reason: stdout/stderr error listeners swallow EIO/EPIPE; polling fallback starts before logging in wl-paste exit handler

title: Popup: right-click Edit/Delete context menu + Del key deletes selected clip
description: Right-click on a clip shows a themed in-page menu with Edit and Delete (no delete confirmation). Edit opens an inline editor (textarea, Save/Ctrl+Enter, Cancel/Esc) writing back via new clips:update IPC (recomputes hash, clears stale content_text; images not editable). Del key deletes the currently selected entry; selection lands on the next item (same index, clamped). Del in a non-empty search input keeps editing text.
close_reason: Verified via Electron renderer harness on the built popup: Del deletes selected row, selection lands on next item, context menu opens, Edit round-trips through clips:update. 94 unit tests pass.

title: About page hardcodes v0.1.0 — read app.getVersion() at runtime
description: about.html has a literal 'v0.1.0' div, so every release shows the wrong version (user upgraded to 0.1.1 and About still said 0.1.0). Add app:version IPC returning app.getVersion(), expose via preload, populate the div at load.

title: Verify installer re-run over an existing install upgrades cleanly
description: Running the NSIS one-click installer when CtrlC is already installed should close the running app, upgrade in place, and relaunch. electron-builder's oneClick NSIS handles app termination and Windows singleton uses a named mutex (auto-released on process death), so no stale-lock issue is expected. Verify empirically with the v0.1.1 installer over v0.1.0.

title: Plain-text paste modifier (hold Ctrl/Shift/Alt while selecting a clip)
description: Holding a configurable modifier (default Ctrl) while selecting a clip (click, Enter, 1-5) pastes stripped plain text: clips:paste gains a plain flag; html clips write contentText || htmlToText(content) as text-only. Settings > Popup gains a 'Plain-text paste modifier' dropdown (Ctrl/Shift/Alt). In-popup Ctrl+Shift+V repointed to the same path.
close_reason: Plain-paste modifier verified end-to-end via ydotool selection (plain-only flavors with Ctrl, both flavors without). Dialog centering follows the proven popup re-assert pattern; visual confirmation on next Settings open.

title: Clear History / delete don't reset in-memory dedup — next copy of same content skipped
description: ClipboardCapture.lastClipHash survives clips:clear and clips:delete, so re-copying the most recently captured content after clearing/deleting is silently dropped. Add resetDedup() and call it from both IPC handlers.
close_reason: resetDedup() wired into clips:clear and clips:delete handlers; capture verified working on the deployed build. Dialog-click flow left for user verification.

title: Popup list: show plain-text preview for HTML clips (paste keeps formatting)
description: HTML clips render as raw markup ('<div ...') in the list, making entries indistinguishable. Display the tag-stripped text for html clips (and search against the same display text); clip content is untouched so pasting stays formatted. Keep the HTML badge. Implemented as one small displayText() helper in popup.ts so it's trivial to tweak or revert.
close_reason: Verified on host via popup screenshot: HTML clips display tag-stripped text with HTML badge; caret code fully removed, config migrates to mouse automatically, gsettings flag reverted.

title: Remove Cursor (caret) popup position mode and AT-SPI tracking code
description: User tested across freshly restarted apps; not reliable enough and not valued. Remove caret.ts, dbus-next dependency, the 'caret' PopupPositionMode, settings dropdown option, and main.ts branches. Config values of popupPosition=caret fall back to default 'mouse' automatically via the existing validation. Also revert the host gsettings toolkit-accessibility flag that was enabled for it.
close_reason: Verified on host via popup screenshot: HTML clips display tag-stripped text with HTML badge; caret code fully removed, config migrates to mouse automatically, gsettings flag reverted.

title: Double-click launcher: single-file executable (AppImage) + application menu entry
description: npm run build:app (electron-builder) already targets AppImage and deb. Deliver: a built AppImage the user can double-click, plus a .desktop menu entry (Utility category) installed to ~/.local/share/applications so it appears in the KDE launcher. Verify the AppImage runs on Bazzite (FUSE availability) and that single-instance + --show-popup still work from the packaged binary (paths in desktop-shortcut.ts/auto-start.ts must use the AppImage path, see process.env.APPIMAGE). Related: installer issue.
close_reason: AppImage built and verified on host: double-click executable runs, captures, opens popup via --show-popup second-instance, and registers the KDE shortcut with the stable $APPIMAGE path. CI publishes it per release.

title: Installer/uninstaller: package app + Wayland integration setup (ydotoold unit, KDE shortcut, autostart)
description: Ship an installable artifact (electron-builder AppImage + deb are already configured in package.json build section). First-run/install must set up what the app needs on Wayland: (1) user-level ydotoold.service (~/.config/systemd/user/, daemon-reload, enable --now) for paste injection — currently hand-created on the dev host; (2) KDE/GNOME global shortcut registration (already done at runtime, keep); (3) autostart entry pointing at the installed binary (auto-start.ts currently points at the dev electron path). Uninstaller must remove: ydotoold unit, kglobalshortcutsrc entries + ctrlc-popup.desktop launcher (unregisterGlobalShortcut exists), autostart entry, KWin helper script file, and optionally ~/.CtrlC data (ask). Consider a --setup/--teardown CLI flag pair on the app binary so any package format can call them.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.1.0: NSIS one-click per-user installer/uninstaller for Windows; AppImage/deb for Linux with first-run ensureYdotoold() self-setup and 'ctrlc --teardown' uninstall (removes DE shortcut, autostart, ydotoold unit, KWin helper; keeps history). CI release workflow publishes all artifacts on tags.

title: Paste on select: selecting a clip pastes into the previously focused window
description: Ditto behavior: choosing a clip should copy it, hide the popup, and synthesize Ctrl+V into the window that had focus. Linux: ydotool (uinput, works on Wayland; Bazzite ships it) with xdotool fallback for XWayland targets. Requires ydotoold running.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on host: popup 440x320 placed 12px below live mouse position; paste path exercised via ydotoold (user service created and running, no-op injection verified); Clear History relocated under Retention right-aligned without label.

title: Popup position modes + setting dropdown (cursor/mouse/center-primary/center-current)
description: Popup should appear just below the mouse pointer by default, clamped to the screen (flip above pointer when near bottom edge). Make popup slightly more compact (440x320). Add a Pop-up Position setting with options: Cursor (text caret), Mouse, Center of Primary Screen, Center of Current Screen. Caret tracking has no portable Linux API — falls back to mouse for now (follow-up: AT-SPI).
close_reason: Implemented and verified on host: popup 440x320 placed 12px below live mouse position; paste path exercised via ydotoold (user service created and running, no-op injection verified); Clear History relocated under Retention right-aligned without label.

title: copyClipToSystem writes image path bytes as PNG; autostart Exec missing app path
description: Image clips store a file path but copyClipToSystem writes Buffer.from(path) with macOS 'public.png' format. Use nativeImage.createFromPath + clipboard.writeImage. auto-start.ts writes Exec=<electron> --silent without the app path, which launches Electron's default app in dev mode.
close_reason: Fixed and verified end-to-end on Bazzite/KDE Wayland: wl-paste watcher captures live copies into the DB; KDE shortcut registered and activated via kglobalaccel D-Bus without re-login.

title: config:update bugs: hotkey change never re-registers; saveConfig drops other settings
description: config is merged before comparing updates.hotkey !== config.hotkey (always false). saveConfig(updates) rewrites config.toml with only the updated keys, losing all other settings (evidenced: config.toml on disk only has autoStart). Fix ordering and save full config.
close_reason: Fixed and verified end-to-end on Bazzite/KDE Wayland: wl-paste watcher captures live copies into the DB; KDE shortcut registered and activated via kglobalaccel D-Bus without re-login.

title: Core: About screen with Serious Geese branding
description:
Build an About dialog/screen showing:
- App name: CtrlC
- Version number
- Description: Cross-platform clipboard manager
- Branding: Serious Geese, LLC
- Link to seriousgeese.dev
- MIT License notice
- GitHub repo link

Acceptance: About screen opens from tray menu and displays correct branding.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add macOS build target and publish a mac release
description: Electron app currently builds for Windows and Linux only. Add a macOS build config (dmg + zip, arm64 + x64) to package.json, add a macos-latest runner to the release workflow, and cut a release with mac artifacts. Builds will be unsigned (no Apple Developer ID) so document the Gatekeeper right-click-open workaround.
close_reason: Shipped v0.1.10 with mac dmg+zip (arm64+x64) built on macos-latest CI; osascript paste wired up; release published with all artifacts.

title: Linux: install a visible start-menu .desktop entry on first run
description: AppImage users have no way to launch CtrlC from the application menu: the app only installs a NoDisplay=true popup-shortcut launcher and (optionally) an autostart entry, so it never appears in the KDE/GNOME menu. On first Linux run, write ~/.local/share/applications/dev.seriousgeese.ctrlc.desktop pointing at launcherParts().execPath with the app icon copied to ~/.local/share/icons, and remove it in teardownLinuxIntegration(). Related: CtrlC-o0e self-update should keep the Exec path stable (~/.local/bin/CtrlC.AppImage).

title: Auto-updater: GitHub release polling with winget and direct-install upgrade paths
description: Add a lightweight update checker that polls the GitHub releases API on startup (30s delay) and every 24h. When a newer version is found: update the tray menu, broadcast to open renderer windows, and show update UI in the About and Settings screens. On Windows show both 'Copy winget command' and 'Open GitHub Releases' options; on other platforms just the releases link.
close_reason: Implemented: GitHub releases poller, tray update item, About banner, Settings footer controls, CODEOWNERS

title: Popup: distinct badge colors for HTML/Image; remove tray 'Copy Last Clip'
description: Badges: TEXT keeps the green theme; HTML gets amber, IMAGE gets magenta (phosphor-terminal companions to green — large hue separation, readable on dark). Tray: drop the redundant 'Copy Last Clip' item and its trailing separator, plus the dead copy-last wiring.
close_reason: Badges verified visually in the render harness (green/amber/magenta, borders); tray menu trimmed and copy-last wiring removed. Tests 94 passing with exit code checked.

title: About window: drop hotkey rows + separators, add Serious Geese logo, fit content
description: Remove the two 'Hotkey…' info rows and both hr separators; add the Serious Geese logo (assets/seriousgeese-logo.jpg, from ~/Downloads) above the 'Serious Geese, LLC' text; size the window so no scrolling is needed (verify via scrollHeight <= innerHeight and a screenshot).
close_reason: Verified by rendering dist/renderer/about.html in a 380x440 useContentSize window: zero overflow, logo displays above Serious Geese, LLC, screenshot inspected.

title: Settings/About dialogs open centered on the primary monitor
description: SettingsManager/AboutManager create windows without a position, so KWin places them arbitrarily. Center both on screen.getPrimaryDisplay().workArea via a shared centerOnPrimary(width,height) helper in popup/position.ts, re-asserting position after map (same pattern as popup showAt).
close_reason: Plain-paste modifier verified end-to-end via ydotool selection (plain-only flavors with Ctrl, both flavors without). Dialog centering follows the proven popup re-assert pattern; visual confirmation on next Settings open.

title: Friendly process name in task manager (shows 'electron')
description: Task managers read the binary name from /proc/<pid>/comm and cmdline; process.title='CtrlC' in main.ts is insufficient (helpers still show electron; comm is truncated/overridden). Dev script uses bash exec -a CtrlC which covers only ps for the wrapper. Real fix: packaged binary named 'ctrlc' via electron-builder (productName/executableName), which renames the executable and all child process rows; also set app.setName('CtrlC') and ensure the popup/settings windows' desktop entry (StartupWMClass=ctrlc) groups correctly in the taskbar. Blocked-by/related: installer issue.
close_reason: Packaged binary is named ctrlc / CtrlC.exe via electron-builder executableName+productName — task managers show CtrlC instead of electron. Verified on Linux (pgrep shows ctrlc processes for the AppImage).

title: Settings: move Clear History under Retention, drop label, right-align
description: Move the Clear History button into the Retention section (logical grouping), remove the 'Delete all saved clips' label, keep the button right-aligned.
close_reason: Implemented and verified on host: popup 440x320 placed 12px below live mouse position; paste path exercised via ydotoold (user service created and running, no-op injection verified); Clear History relocated under Retention right-aligned without label.

title: Settings: Clear History button with confirm/cancel prompt
description: Add a Clear History button to the settings panel. Native confirmation dialog (Clear History / Cancel); on confirm delete all clips rows and saved image files in ~/.CtrlC/Clips.
close_reason: Popup refresh verified live on the host: clip count updated from stale 19 to 30 on show, marker string captured post-startup appears in DB and list. Clear History wired via clips:clear IPC + native dialog; clearAllClips covered by unit tests (84 passing).

title: UX issues: settings close button, hotkey display, menu bar, ESC dismiss, window icons
description:
Several UX issues found during testing:

**Settings Window:**
- ✅ "Close" button overlays content and looks bad - should be in header or removed
- ✅ "Toggle popup" hotkey text is truncated ("CommandOrControl+Backqu") and too long
- ✅ "Appearance" section title doesn't match its contents (should be "Retention" or "General")
- ✅ Hotkey recording has no cancel mechanism - Esc and X button needed
- ✅ Window icon shows "W" instead of app icon

**Popup:**
- ✅ "Esc to dismiss" shown but Esc doesn't actually dismiss the popup

**Menu Bar:**
- ✅ Edit, View, Window menu items present but unused - should be removed

**Platform Display:**
- ✅ Hotkey display should show "Ctrl" for Linux/Windows, "Cmd" for macOS instead of "CommandOrControl"

**Status: All fixes implemented and pushed. Awaiting user verification.**

title: Linux update script: replace AppImage in-place and relaunch
description: When the user clicks 'GitHub Releases' from the update banner on Linux, ideally we could offer a self-update path: download the new AppImage from the release URL, replace ~/.local/bin/CtrlC.AppImage, chmod +x, kill the running instance, and relaunch. This avoids the manual download-move-relaunch cycle. Should respect CTRLC_APPIMAGE_PATH env override. Falls back to opening the browser if the download fails or the install path is non-standard.

title: Windows code signing: SmartScreen 'Unknown publisher' warning on installers
description: Unsigned NSIS exe triggers SmartScreen. User workaround: More info > Run anyway, or Properties > Unblock. Durable options: (1) SignPath Foundation free OSS signing — apply, integrate into the release workflow (best free path; consistent cert lets reputation persist across releases); (2) Azure Trusted Signing ~$10/mo; (3) EV cert for instant reputation (expensive). Also consider winget submission (microsoft/winget-pkgs PR) — winget installs skip browser Mark-of-the-Web so users bypass SmartScreen entirely.

title: Re-attempt Electron upgrade beyond v33 (42 shows no windows on KDE Wayland)
description: Electron 42.5 displays no windows on Bazzite/KDE Plasma Wayland: native-Wayland surfaces render (capturePage returns content) but are never composited by KWin; forced ozone-platform=x11 crash-loops the GPU process (exit 139) and creates no X11 windows. Electron 33 works via XWayland. Bisect majors 34-42 with a minimal BrowserWindow repro (red background, screenshot diff) to find the last working version, and watch Electron/Chromium upstream for the KWin color-management / wp_color_manager fix (startup logged 'Unable to set image transfer function' from wayland_wp_color_manager). Blocked-by nothing; low urgency since 33 works.

title: Re-copying existing content should bump clip to top (Ditto behavior)
description: clipExistsByHash skips inserting when content was ever captured before, so re-copied items keep their old created_at and sort position. Ditto moves them to the top. Update created_at on hash match instead of skipping.
close_reason: Verified live on host: copying A, B, A-again leaves a single A row at the top of history. htmlToText covered by unit tests (92 passing) including multi-line digest case.

title: Paste synthesis for Windows (SendInput) and macOS (CGEvent)
description: synthesizePaste() is Linux-only (ydotool/xdotool). Add native key injection for Windows and macOS when those platforms are targeted.
close_reason: Windows paste via PowerShell SendKeys implemented (macOS still TODO, left noted in paste.ts). Runtime verification pending user's Windows machine.

title: Caret ('Cursor') popup position: real text-caret tracking via AT-SPI
description: The Cursor option currently falls back to mouse position. Investigate AT-SPI2 (org.a11y.atspi) caret-tracking to place the popup at the text caret of the focused app.
close_reason: Implemented via AT-SPI with dbus-next. Verified on host: popup maps 12px below Konsole's live caret with Qt Wayland origin fix-up. Chromium apps emit after next launch (toolkit-accessibility enabled). Falls back to mouse placement whenever the caret is unknown or untrustworthy.

title: P4: macOS integration — Finder/LaunchBar-style integration
description: Add macOS-specific integrations: menu bar entry, keyboard shortcut via System Settings, Spotlight-like search.

title: P4: Format filters — toggle text/HTML/image/binary capture
description: Allow user to disable capture of specific clip types (e.g., skip images, skip binary files, skip HTML). Configurable via settings.

title: P4: Optimize image storage with thumbnail compression
description: Store image thumbnails (128px) instead of full-resolution images to save disk space. Configurable max storage size. JPEG compression at 70% quality.
